scope: Review of Harness Engineering Studio and CyberLACE Autonomous Security Training Loop against code, checkpoints and live endpoints.

findings.title: Autopilot run state is in-memory until final checkpoint
findings.detail: cyberlace_training_runs is a process-local dict. autopilot-start stores queued/running status only in memory, and autopilot-status reads only that dict. If the backend restarts mid-campaign, the UI loses the run and there is no per-step persisted run state to resume from.

findings.title: No global concurrency/budget gate for autonomous campaigns
findings.detail: autopilot-start accepts up to 1000 cycles and starts a background task for every request. The UI disables its own button, but another tab or direct API call can start multiple campaigns and saturate the runtime.

findings.title: Harness accepts client supplied baseUrl for server-side requests
findings.detail: generate-run/autopilot-start/autopilot-run accept baseUrl from the request payload and then the backend makes HTTP calls to it. In local-only development this is convenient, but if exposed it is an SSRF-style risk and can point the harness away from the intended local runtime.

findings.title: Worker process check can false-fail under unrelated active workers
findings.detail: process_check builds a broad pgrep OR pattern including workers.codex_worker and VISTA_AGENT_SESSION_ID. Any unrelated live worker can make mustNotStartCodex fail even if the current case did not start a worker.

findings.title: Documentation still describes continuous UI mode that current component does not expose
findings.detail: The backend supports continuous=true, but HarnessEngineeringStudio hardcodes continuous=false and the UI text now says it runs an exact task count and stops. This is a docs/UI contract mismatch.

summary: The layer exists and is wired end-to-end: UI -> backend endpoints -> training loop -> runtime session -> reports/checkpoints -> Safety Learning memory. The main gap is durability/governance for long autonomous campaigns.
nextRecommendation: Before running long campaigns, persist live autopilot run state per cycle and add a single-run/budget gate. Then align docs/UI for continuous mode.
